greet.text: She's leaning against a dead line machine with her arms folded, mirrors reflecting torchlight and the long math of her own history. "Sally's for business. It's Molly tonight." The blades are away; what's out is older and quieter. "I knew a cowboy once who went into the dark like this. I didn't get a goodbye that time. So this time I'm collecting one in advance."

options.text: "The Finn told Kumiko about him. About Case."

case.text: "Finn always did talk too much for a dead guy." But there's no heat in it, and the mirrors dip a centimeter. "Case got out. Meat, wife, kids, the whole gravity well. Me, I kept the road." She studies you — the lace, the stretcher, the humming slab. "He'd have understood you two, though. The thing you and your boy found in the wires — that's the same thing we all went in after. You're just the first ones it wanted BACK."

blessing.text: "Blessings." She snorts, softly, at the pretension of it — then unfolds her arms and gives you one anyway, razorgirl-style: "The ghost that squeezed us all is in there waiting to be paid off. The Finn's filed what voids her paper; the dead lady of the cemeteries holds the court. You walk in like you own the place, 'cause as of tonight, kid — you two DO." A pause, and then, quieter than you'll ever tell anyone: "Be happy. One of us should get to be. Consider it an ORDER."

options.text: "Yes ma'am. — Watch the door for me?" (end)

bye.text: "Always do," says Molly Millions, and turns to face the Factory doors and the whole world beyond them — the last watch of the longest-serving guardian the matrix ever had, standing easy, mirrors full of torchlight.
